methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Mitra Chem, a Series B climate and energy company founded by Vivas Kumar and Will Fitzgerald, has raised $60M+ to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of 9 peers (1 public, 8 private) operating in the same sector and stage, including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc.

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach uses the $60M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $114M. The peer-median method anchors to six same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $355M, though with substantial dispersion—the range spans $120M to $5.0B, reflecting high variance in climate-tech valuations at this stage.

The triangulation of these two methods produces a valuation range of $97M to $408M, with a median of $234M. The wide spread reflects the material uncertainty inherent in peer-based valuation within an early-stage, capital-intensive sector where comparable companies show significant heterogeneity in technology maturity, capital efficiency, and growth trajectory. The stage-progression method anchors conservatively to observed funding; the peer median better captures market pricing but exhibits outsized influence from outliers like Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B. Key uncertainties include technology validation progress, capital efficiency relative to peers, and path to commercialization.
